mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation signals eroding purchasing power and forces bond markets to demand higher yields as compensation. A steady increase in CPIAUCSL over several months compresses real yields upward, pushing nominal 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the directional accuracy of positive CPIAUCSL year-over-year changes predicting yield increases remains below 55% over any rolling 2-year window, or if the rolling 3-month correlation between CPI change and 20-day yield change is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below a critical threshold such as 4.5%, it indicates a tightening labor market that fuels wage growth expectations and inflationary pressure. Bond markets preemptively sell off, driving 10-year yields higher in the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If after the trigger is active, the 10-year yield produces a negative 20-day return in more than 60% of instances over any consecutive 3-year period in the test data.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) signals that monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This artificially depresses short-term rates and forces investors to seek higher long-term yields, driving the 10-year yield up over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 5-year correlation between the real rate and subsequent 60-day yield change is not significantly negative, or if the signal produces a positive yield change in less than 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend GDP growth (GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeding 3%), rising industrial production indicates strong capacity utilization and potential supply constraints. Markets anticipate higher inflation and tighter monetary policy, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next month.
failure_condition: If in the designated regime, a positive INDPRO month-over-month change leads to a decline in the 10-year yield in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated level of housing starts (HOUST) signals robust residential investment and overall demand strength. This implies future economic growth and borrowing needs, pushing long-term yields upward over a quarterly horizon as the economy absorbs credit.
failure_condition: If the 3-year rolling correlation between HOUST levels and subsequent 60-day yield changes is non-positive, or if the long-only signal based on HOUST above its 12-month average yields a negative hit rate over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) exceed a high threshold (approximately 475,000 millions of dollars), it signals exceptionally strong consumer spending that fuels growth expectations. Bond markets react by pricing in higher rates to head off overheating, lifting the 10-year yield over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If triggered instances are followed by a negative 20-day yield change in more than 55% of occurrences over a rolling 3-year window, or if the average yield change after the trigger is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll gains (PAYEMS 3-month change above 500,000) and falling unemployment (UNRATE 3-month change negative) jointly indicate a labour market that is tightening rapidly. This combination intensifies wage-inflation fears and leads bond markets to push yields higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces a positive 20-day yield change in less than 60% of occurrences over any 30-month rolling window, or if the interaction term in a regression of yield changes on the two series is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a rise in the federal funds rate is interpreted as a normalisation move rather than a panic response. Markets digest the hike as positive for the economy and raise term premiums, leading to a short-term increase in the 10-year yield over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If in the low-inflation regime, FOMC meeting dates with a rate hike are followed by a 5-day yield decline in more than 50% of cases, or if the average 5-day yield change after hikes in the regime is not positive.
